content.problemStatement:
You are an administrator of a popular quiz website. Every day, you hold a quiz on the website.

There are NNN users (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM admins (numbered N+1N+1N+1 through N+MN+MN+M). For each quiz, each user is allowed to attempt it at most once. Sometimes, admins attempt the quizzes for testing purposes too; an admin may attempt a quiz any number of times.

On a certain day, the quiz was attempted KKK times. For each iii (1≤i≤K1 \le i \le K1≤i≤K), the iii-th of these attempts was made by the person with number LiL_iLi​. Find all the users who attempted the quiz more than once, in order to disqualify them.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains three integers N, M and K. The second line contains K space‑separated integers L1 … LK denoting the IDs of the persons who attempted the quiz (users are numbered 1…N, admins N+1…N+M).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line starting with D, the number of users who attempted the quiz more than once. If D>0, follow D by a space and D distinct user IDs in increasing order. If D=0 output only 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: only one attempt, no user repeats.\nTest case 2: user 1 appears twice.\nTest case 3: users 2 and 5 appear more than once; admins may repeat.

solution.approach: For each test case we need to know which user IDs (1..N) appear at least twice among the K attempts. Admin IDs are ignored for the disqualification rule. We can keep a frequency array of size N+1 (index 0 unused). While reading the K attempts, if the current ID x is <=N we increment freq[x]. After all attempts we scan the array from 1 to N and collect all indices with freq[i]>1. Because we scan in order the collected IDs are already sorted. Finally we output the count and the list. The algorithm runs in O(K+N) time per test case and uses O(N) additional memory.
